title: The Fuse
timelinePosition: Runs parallel to Chapter 12. While Jaina is already inside Scholomance after Chapter 10's descent, Sylvanas and Cyndia ride south from the Chapter 9 split to brief Arthas, then turn north again when Jaina's trail goes dark.
openLocation: Eastweald road south toward Hearthglen and Arthas's field camp.
transport: Mounted travel on Thalassian horses south through the Eastweald to Arthas's camp, then hard north again toward Caer Darrow.
immediateObjective: Deliver the plague intelligence to Arthas and bring military power to bear before the Cult rolls farther ahead.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
Keep Jaina off-page and simultaneous; she appears as missing capability and exposed risk, not as a live conversation partner.
No explicit Mal'Ganis identification or resolution of the old seller / repeated farewell pattern.
No claim that Arthas understands the full mechanism; Sylvanas can brief vector, spread, and logistics more cleanly than the deeper body-side theory.

povKnowsAtOpen: Chapter 9 split the investigation cleanly: Jaina took the Barov / Scholomance thread while Sylvanas and Cyndia took the Arthas / Hearthglen thread.
The contaminated grain network is organized, regional, and embedded in ordinary supply infrastructure.
Barov holdings sit at the center of the surviving paperwork, but ownership is not yet proof of guilt.
Lyriel is dead, the formation is reduced, and the team is now undermanned for both field combat and arcane assessment.
Their field work is catching the plague earlier than institutions can catch it, but still one step behind the rollout because every identified shipment has already landed.
Cyndia's Chapter 9 breadcrumb points to Arthas at Hearthglen, and the same formal farewell now sits in Sylvanas's file beside the old seller without yet becoming proof.
Without Jaina present, Sylvanas can brief vector, spread, and logistics but not the deeper host-conditioning theory.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How Arthas will react once the warning lands.
Jaina's exact status on the Barov / Scholomance line.
Whether the Barovs are complicit or merely penetrated.
Whether the old seller / repeated farewell pattern matters.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No claim that Arthas understands the mechanism just because he understands the threat.
No suggestion that Jaina's thread is happening later; it is already running in parallel.
No resolution of the old seller / repeated farewell clue.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Arthas is mobilized toward Hearthglen with conviction stronger than nuance.
Uther has named the flaw in Arthas even though nothing changes here.
Recall pressure and evidence-erasure are actively collapsing support behind the field investigation.
Sylvanas and Cyndia turn north toward Caer Darrow while Jaina's interior thread continues in parallel.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: If Sylvanas can aim the right humans at the plague fast enough, this can still be treated as a border crisis carried south by necessity.
pov.surfaceObjective: Point institutional force at the plague before the Cult rolls farther ahead than two rangers can track.
pov.pressureSource: The formation is cut down, Jaina is gone, and every human power center Sylvanas could use is attached to people she does not trust to read the threat correctly.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Sylvanas still half hopes speed, command instinct, and the right military handoff can cover the missing arcane read.
pov.chapterTest: She has to brief Arthas, judge Uther, and decide whether using them helps more than it hurts when the wrong solution can still arrive with banners and authority.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep pretending this is somebody else's war to wake up, or cross the line fully and steer human power anyway, even knowing it may move wrong.
pov.endStateShift: By chapter close the border fiction is dead. Sylvanas is acting inside Lordaeron's collapse, not merely reporting on it.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 13 inherits a Sylvanas who is already deep in the wrong country, short one partner, and moving on choices she would rather not have had to make.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Missing arcane sweep and the voice that would tell Sylvanas when the obvious route is magical bait. Sylvanas feels the hole in the work before she lets herself feel it anywhere else.
throughPov.povMisreadAtOpen: Sylvanas knows Jaina matters, but she still half imagines raw judgment and speed can manage around the missing magical read.
throughPov.correctionEarnedHere: Without Jaina, Sylvanas keeps seeing where the wrong solution becomes attractive. The absence teaches her what Jaina had been preventing.
throughPov.interactionRule: Keep Jaina off-page and simultaneous. She appears as missing capability and unanswered risk, not as a convenient source of new information.

team.label: partnership
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The absence exposes the weak side of each woman's skill set instead of proving either can replace the other.
team.namingAttitudeRule: Jaina's absence and Lyriel's ghost should live in formation changes, route choices, and caution, not speeches.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Do not flatten this into missing-a-friend language. The point is that every route decision is worse without the mage who priced magical terrain.

entries.rule: Off-page inside the Barov / Scholomance thread. Do not imply her scenes are later in time than this chapter.
With Sylvanas throughout; the right flank still carries Lyriel's ghost.
Human command node Sylvanas chooses to activate.
Secondary command read whose warning matters later even if it changes nothing here.
Gatekeeper into Arthas's camp and the military face of Lordaeron's chain of command.
Off-page institutional pressure through relays and dispatches.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 12 continues the simultaneous Scholomance interior thread. Chapter 13 opens once Sylvanas reaches Caer Darrow after the northbound redirect made here.

factsLockedForChapter12: Arthas has been mobilized toward Hearthglen with full conviction and inadequate nuance.
Uther's warning about Arthas is now planted in the file.
Recall pressure and evidence-erasure are active political threats behind the field operation.
The old seller / repeated farewell pattern remains unresolved and still lacks proof.
Chapter 12 is simultaneous interior time while Sylvanas and Cyndia are already moving north toward Caer Darrow.
factsLockedForChapter13: Sylvanas and Cyndia are moving north toward Caer Darrow.
Arthas remains the launched instrument on the southern line, out of Sylvanas's direct control.
Chapter 13 is the arrival created by this redirect, not a fresh decision to go after Jaina.
factsNotYetEarned: Hearthglen and Stratholme outcomes on page.
Any repair of the split partnership.

scenes.title: The Road Alone
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the reduced formation as live deficit, then prove the territory is already being administered by the plague.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina is on the Barov line alone; the southbound thread must deliver institutional force fast enough to matter.

enrichment.detail: The Eastweald is Lordaeron's fertile grain country — wet, mild, road-laced farmland that later becomes the Western Plaguelands. Using orchards, harvest fields, and worked roads under plague pressure keeps the scene grounded in a place that should still read as productive human countryside.

beats.emotion: The absence is operational first. Sylvanas feels Jaina and Lyriel both in the places they are not.
beats.dilemma: Slow down for the missing capabilities, or keep speed and accept that neither ranger can read the magical terrain the way Jaina would.
beats.decision: Keep speed and compensate with caution at every place a mage would have priced automatically.
newOnPageInformation: Cyndia checks right twice where Lyriel used to live in the formation.
Sylvanas now pauses at magical chokepoints she would once have crossed without a thought.
The wound from Chapter 9 is healing, not healed, and Jaina's bandage is still doing its work.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether speed can outrun what the remaining two-woman formation can no longer read.
sensoryAnchors: creak of bow against Sylvanas's back
hoofbeats too few for the formation this road should carry
cold air pulling at the healing wound when she twists to check the line
beats.transitionIntent: landscape-answer: the road itself starts proving what kind of country they are moving through now.
beats.plantedThread: Cyndia's doubled right-flank check is now a standing behavioral consequence of Lyriel's death.
beats.verifierNotes: Jaina stays off-page and simultaneous here. Let the missing arcane read and Cyndia's doubled right-flank check carry the prior losses without re-explaining them.

beats.goal: Move south through contested territory toward Arthas's reported position.
beats.conflict: The Eastweald shows plague occupation without open battle: silent villages, ruined harvests, watchers who do not need to engage.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas and Cyndia keep moving, but the country they ride through proves the land is already lost in ways cavalry cannot answer.

newOnPageInformation: Whole settlements are being administered by aftermath rather than openly fought over.
Cult watchers observe and withdraw because the land is already working for them.
The smell of treated grain is now part of the road itself.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How far south the contamination has already spread.
Whether Hearthglen and Arthas's camp are already consuming compromised stores.
sensoryAnchors: grain left rotting on the stalk
dog at a gate too listless to bark
sweet-chemical smell rolling off closed granaries
beats.transitionIntent: scale-pressure: the country forces Sylvanas to think in institutional rather than ranger-sized responses.
beats.verifierModification: Aligned the outcome shape with the beat goal: they do keep moving south, so the cost lands as yes-but rather than no-and.

beats.dilemma: Keep the threat on ranger scale, or admit the operation now requires a prince and an army.
beats.decision: Go to Arthas. Two scouts cannot dismantle a kingdom-wide plague infrastructure.
beats.valueShift: occupied plague country -> institutional escalation chosen
newOnPageInformation: Lyriel's death has become part of Sylvanas's argument for scale.
The split from Chapter 9 is already bending the border-defense Lie even if Sylvanas does not say so aloud.
Cyndia's Hearthglen breadcrumb is now the operational target, not background oddity.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas becomes tool or obstacle once he hears the truth.
sensoryAnchors: evening road flattening under hard pace
Cyndia riding tighter than a full formation would allow
the feeling of making a clean decision for ugly reasons
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-pull: the road delivers them to the prince's camp and the visible war Lordaeron understands.

scenes.title: The Prince's Camp
scenes.sceneFunction: Put Sylvanas's careful field intelligence in front of a military machine built for the wrong kind of enemy.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The plague picture is clear enough to brief; the human command structure has not yet absorbed it.

enrichment.detail: Hearthglen is a fortified northern town built around real defensive infrastructure — keep, town hall, mage tower, forge, scout tower, stables, and the funnel of Hearthglen Pass into nearby woods. That makes Arthas's camp read like a genuine military staging ground at the edge of a defendable town, not an abstract field bivouac.

beats.goal: Assess Arthas's camp and confirm that real institutional power is present.
beats.conflict: Everything about the camp is configured for visible war — cavalry lanes, outward defenses, field-forge tempo — while the plague works through bread, not charges.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas finds the force she came for, but it is aimed at the wrong shape of threat.

newOnPageInformation: Arthas commands a substantial, disciplined field force with serious reach.
The camp's medical tents are already treating sickness the Light does not easily answer.
Sylvanas can see both the military usefulness of the camp and its blindness to the enemy in the grain.
Sylvanas can feel where Jaina would have read the camp's sickness pattern faster and with more precision than anyone here.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anyone here can be redirected before the plague outruns the briefing.
sensoryAnchors: hammering at the field forge
mud, banners, and cavalry smell layered over healer's incense
low uncertainty running through a camp that knows something is wrong but not what
beats.transitionIntent: protocol-pressure: Sylvanas now has to pass through rank and ceremony without losing urgency.
beats.verifierNotes: Arthas is not a fresh command node in the book. Treat this camp as the evolution of the earlier refusal, now re-entered from Sylvanas's lane.

beats.goal: Secure an audience with Prince Arthas quickly enough for the warning to matter.
beats.conflict: Diplomatic protocol and human command structure stand between urgency and the prince.

outcome.summary: Access is granted, but only after Sylvanas endures the chain-of-command friction she hates.

newOnPageInformation: Falric is efficient, loyal, and defined by service to Arthas.
The camp's rumor-line already knows the healers are failing against something strange.
Even at speed, this army still processes information through rank, permission, and optics.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas will hear the warning as a command brief or as another unwelcome theory.
sensoryAnchors: Falric's holy plate ringing differently from common steel
healers' tents visible beyond the checkpoint
Sylvanas containing open impatience because the audience matters more than the insult
beats.transitionIntent: formal-barrier-falls: the warning finally reaches the right tent.
beats.dialogueGuidance: Falric stays crisp, deferential, and military; Sylvanas answers in command language rather than diplomatic smoothing.
beats.verifierNotes: Falric is newly on-page here; do not imply any prior personal history with Sylvanas.

beats.goal: Present the plague intelligence in a form Arthas can act on.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas has the strategic picture, but not Jaina's full arcane mechanism; she must brief through personal history and incomplete explanatory depth.

outcome.summary: Arthas listens seriously, but he receives the what and where more cleanly than the how.

newOnPageInformation: The grain network overlays the same map Arthas has been using for visible war.
Sylvanas withholds Kel'Thuzad, Jaina's location, and the deepest mechanism because those belong to the other thread or remain incomplete.
Without Jaina, Sylvanas can describe what the plague does and where it moves, but not the full body-side answer.
Arthas responds to Sylvanas when she speaks in command language rather than warning language.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Arthas's reaction will be proportional to the threat instead of merely decisive.
sensoryAnchors: campaign map filling with blue plague marks over red war marks
canvas-walled tent suddenly too small for the scale of the intelligence
Arthas's attention sharpening the moment the data stops sounding theoretical
beats.transitionIntent: conversion-pull: Arthas starts moving on the intelligence before Sylvanas has finished unfolding it.
beats.verifierNotes: Sylvanas can brief vector, spread, and logistics, but not the deeper body-side mechanism Jaina is chasing below Scholomance.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beats 4-6 are all scene beats with yes-but outcomes, so camp access, gatekeeping, and briefing need clearly different pressure textures in prose.

scenes.title: The Conversion
scenes.sceneFunction: Turn a correct briefing into a dangerous mobilization and plant the warning Uther sees too late.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas has the correct target at last, but not the full mechanism or pacing wisdom behind it.
scenes.arcPressure: This is the chapter's main stance test: Sylvanas needs institutional force badly enough to accept the very commander she is already starting to read as dangerous.

enrichment.detail: Uther's warning carries unusual weight because he is Arthas's mentor and the first paladin, while Falric represents the King's Guard loyalty chain closest to the prince. The scene works best when that nested hierarchy is visible: old teacher naming the flaw, loyal lieutenant translating certainty into immediate action.

beats.goal: Finish the briefing and activate institutional power against the Cult.
beats.conflict: Arthas does not need steering once the pattern clicks; he turns certainty into motion faster than the intelligence can finish unfolding.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets the army moving, but it moves at Arthas's tempo rather than the plague's logic.

beats.valueShift: attention engaged -> power mobilized at dangerous speed
newOnPageInformation: Hearthglen's scattered reports instantly slot into place for Arthas once grain becomes the frame.
Arthas is the kind of commander who decides before the full analytical foundation is built.
Sylvanas recognizes the danger of a man who understands threat faster than nuance.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What civilian cost Arthas's speed will create once he meets a plague he cannot simply charge.
sensoryAnchors: Arthas's hand claiming the map instead of studying it
orders beginning before the discussion has technically ended
the command tent changing temperature when certainty turns into mobilization
beats.transitionIntent: mentor-read: Uther sees the shift immediately and becomes the only person willing to name the prince's flaw.

beats.dilemma: Trust the necessary hammer because it exists, or weigh Uther's warning even though it changes nothing operationally right now.
beats.decision: Let mobilization stand and file Uther's assessment for the future.
beats.valueShift: power mobilized at dangerous speed -> named flaw in the prince
newOnPageInformation: Uther sees Arthas as someone who cannot retreat from a battle he thinks he must win.
The mentor has already recognized the catastrophe vector even if he cannot stop it.
Sylvanas understands the warning well enough to be chilled by it and still not countermand it.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anyone in Lordaeron can still moderate Arthas once he has chosen a target.
sensoryAnchors: two veteran commanders speaking outside canvas instead of inside ceremony
Uther's eyes carrying foreknowledge rather than panic
camp motion rising behind them while the warning lands and goes nowhere
beats.transitionIntent: mobilization-release: the prince's column rides before doubt can accumulate around him.
beats.plantedThread: Uther's assessment is the file that cashes out later at Hearthglen and Stratholme.
beats.verifierNotes: Uther's diagnosis is newly established in-file here; do not imply Sylvanas has heard this warning before.

beats.goal: Observe the consequence of the strategic play and confirm the army is actually moving.
beats.conflict: Success feels wrong because the plague is not an enemy that yields to cavalry tempo.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets exactly the mobilization she wanted, and immediately feels the shape of the mistake she may have launched.

beats.valueShift: named flaw in the prince -> fuse lit
newOnPageInformation: Arthas can turn a camp inside out within the hour once conviction lands.
The army now rides toward Hearthglen under the prince's unbroken certainty.
Sylvanas feels, without yet proving it, that she has handed catastrophe a prince-shaped spear.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Where this fuse ends once it leaves Hearthglen.
sensoryAnchors: dust from six hundred horses hanging in autumn light
struck tents and rolling wagons replacing the earlier camp sprawl
the road swallowing Arthas faster than thought can catch him
beats.transitionIntent: aftermath-freeze: the strategic play is complete, which opens space for institutional backlash and the more urgent personal thread.

sceneLevelWarnings: Beat 7 and beat 9 are both successful mobilization beats with different costs. Keep Uther's interstitial warning doing real tonal and structural separation so the scene does not blur into one long acceleration.

scenes.title: The Redirect
scenes.sceneFunction: Tighten the political vise, then make Jaina's silence the new highest-priority emergency.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Arthas is in motion and the broader response has been handed to human command.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas has to admit that the launched human response does not end her responsibility; Jaina's exposure instantly outranks the strategy she just set in motion.

enrichment.detail: Caer Darrow is not a simple manor revisit but an island keep in Darrowmere Lake, with Scholomance in the crypt structure beneath it. That means the northbound turn at chapter close points toward mist, water, and a fortified necromantic site rather than a quick inland pickup.

beats.dilemma: Keep operating as though field evidence can still defend itself, or admit that institutions are collapsing the operation from behind.
beats.decision: Treat Arthas as the launched instrument and keep the field, accepting that the fight is now inside Lordaeron's collapse rather than outside it.
newOnPageInformation: Verification teams back home are finding cleaned sites and vanishing evidence.
Lor'themar is losing ground against recall pressure.
Bureaucracy is becoming a second front instead of a support structure.
Sylvanas now has to keep acting inside Lordaeron's failure state, not from the safety of a border fiction.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How long until recall pressure becomes operational fact rather than threat.
sensoryAnchors: Lor'themar's handwriting telling a story all by itself
dispatch parchment still warm from relay hands
the feeling of being attacked by process rather than blades
beats.transitionIntent: override-signal: Jaina's line stops being theoretical risk and becomes the urgent thread demanding immediate movement.
beats.verifierModification: Strengthened beat 10 so recall pressure and evidence erasure land as their own consequence, earning the chapter's arc shift before the Jaina relay arrives.
beats.verifierNotes: Recall pressure and evidence erasure are escalations of the chapter9-10 institutional squeeze, not a fresh bureaucracy subplot.

beats.goal: React to intelligence that Jaina entered Caer Darrow and has not returned.
beats.conflict: The Arthas thread is strategically live, but the partner thread has become a confirmed exposure inside the enemy's core.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas hands the broad response off to Arthas and pivots north at once, but she rides toward a problem she cannot yet read.

beats.valueShift: political vise tightening -> northbound recovery mission
newOnPageInformation: Farstrider relays confirm Jaina entered the Barov estate, crossed to Caer Darrow, and did not come out.
Sylvanas reprioritizes instantly, proving that the partnership now outranks the prince-thread in field urgency.
Chapter 11 and Chapter 12 are explicitly parallel rather than consecutive.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether she is compromised, trapped, or simply too deep to leave.
sensoryAnchors: bow back in Sylvanas's hand the moment the direction changes
horses turning north while Arthas's dust still hangs to the south
Cyndia reading the order before Sylvanas has to say it twice
beats.transitionIntent: parallel-handoff: Chapter 12 stays inside the room Jaina entered; Chapter 13 opens when Sylvanas reaches that room.
beats.verifierNotes: Keep this relay locked to external observation only: Jaina entered the estate, crossed to Caer Darrow, and did not come back out. Sylvanas does not know Jaina's interior state below.

warnings: Beats 4-7 still run four consecutive scene beats without a sequel, so camp assessment, gate friction, briefing, and mobilization must land as clearly different pressure textures in prose.

Once beat 2 is aligned to its actual result shape, all seven scene beats land yes-but. The chapter survives that repetition only if each beat changes the scale and cost of the same underlying success.
Beats 8-10 all darken the file, so beat 10 has to land as its own political consequence instead of reading like a mere runway into beat 11.
Beat 1 opens on a sequel. Keep the prose entry hard in mounted motion, wound sensation, and altered formation so the chapter still arrives in-scene rather than in recap.

chapterBridge: Intentional parallel branch from chapter10's ending: Jaina has just gone below Scholomance on permission she does not control, while Chapter 11 cuts sideways to the other half of the chapter9 split, where Sylvanas and Cyndia are already riding the southbound Hearthglen road. The orienting anchor is absence — missing magical read, missing partner, broken formation — and the closing relay stays external only, confirming that Jaina entered Caer Darrow and never re-emerged without claiming Sylvanas knows anything about what happened below.

craftResearch.topic: opening hooks in travel-heavy fantasy chapters
craftResearch.findings: Open in motion with an active pressure, not retrospective explanation. Travel scenes hold when the road itself is already posing the next problem through sensation, deficit, or time pressure.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: travel-scene and chapter-hook craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Action-heavy chapters can lean scene-dominant, but a brief reaction beat after each major surge keeps momentum sharp instead of numbing. Sequel beats 1, 3, 8, and 10 are the breathing spaces that keep this chapter from flattening.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: scene and sequel structure articles

craftResearch.topic: military briefing scenes as conflict, not exposition
craftResearch.findings: A briefing scene works when hierarchy, urgency, and disagreement convert information into pressure. The Arthas sequence was judged on whether rank friction and misprioritized action changed the chapter, not merely delivered lore.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: chapter-structure and suspense craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: A parallel cut needs a clean orienting anchor so the reader lands without mistaking simultaneity for delay. Here that anchor is Jaina's absence and the split-road state carried from chapters 9 and 10.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: chapter-transition and suspense craft guidance

craftResearch.findings: Strong chapter endings close the immediate decision while widening the next danger. Beat 11 works because Sylvanas makes a decisive turn north into a problem the chapter deliberately does not solve.
craftResearch.source: Web research synthesis: chapter-ending craft guidance

beatAudit.pacing: Medium opening beat; the sequel works because it lands inside mounted motion and altered formation rather than pausing for recap.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas chooses speed under acknowledged deficit, which keeps her active instead of reflective-only.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of the chapter9 split and Lyriel-loss formation damage.
beatAudit.transition: The landscape-answer handoff lands cleanly in beat 2, where the road itself proves what country they are in now.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium-high escalation; this is the chapter's first world-level pressure beat.
beatAudit.character: POV stays limited to what Sylvanas can read from land, watchers, and aftermath rather than omniscient plague knowledge.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of established Eastweald hostile-ground continuity, not a fresh wasteland reveal.
beatAudit.transition: Scale-pressure coherently pushes Sylvanas into the institutional choice of beat 3.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium hinge beat; the sequel gives the chapter a needed decision crest before camp logistics begin.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas actively reclassifies the crisis from ranger-scale to state-scale.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of the Hearthglen / Arthas thread planted on the chapter9 ridge.
beatAudit.transition: Arrival-pull lands logically in the visible-war machine of beat 4.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium reset with pressure intact; it starts the camp run without bleeding off urgency.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas reads the camp through mismatch — useful force aimed at the wrong enemy.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of her earlier Arthas encounter and command distrust from chapter6.
beatAudit.transition: Protocol-pressure correctly narrows the scene into gatekeeping at beat 5.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium friction beat; chain-of-command delay varies the camp sequence instead of duplicating beat 4.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas endures protocol because the audience matters more than pride, preserving agency.
beatAudit.continuity: New in-file through Falric and this specific camp gate interaction.
beatAudit.transition: Formal-barrier-falls carries directly into the briefing room of beat 6.

beatAudit.pacing: High informational pressure; the beat works because the briefing is contested action, not static exposition.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas calibrates what she can say, what she withholds, and what she cannot honestly explain without Jaina.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of the command-refusal lane: the warning is sharper now, but still incomplete at the mechanism level.
beatAudit.transition: Conversion-pull is coherent because Arthas starts moving before the nuance is finished.

beatAudit.pacing: High midchapter turn; this is where the chapter changes from briefing to consequence.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas gets the army moving and recognizes the wrong tempo at the same moment.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of Arthas's chapter6 priority flaw rather than a new personality read.
beatAudit.transition: Mentor-read lands cleanly in Uther's sequel beat.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium breathing beat after the mobilization surge; necessary sequel before the final launch.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas actively lets the mobilization stand despite understanding the warning.
beatAudit.continuity: New on-page information through Uther's explicit diagnosis of Arthas.
beatAudit.transition: Mobilization-release lands as direct mounted consequence in beat 9.

beatAudit.pacing: High aftermath crest; success curdles immediately, which keeps the chapter from resolving on triumph.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas owns the sense that she may have lit the fuse herself.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of the live Arthas instrument thread set up on the ridge.
beatAudit.transition: Aftermath-freeze opens enough room for the political squeeze of beat 10.

beatAudit.pacing: Medium-high consequence beat; it must land distinctly so the chapter earns its arc shift before the final hook.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas stays active under institutional collapse, choosing to keep the field instead of retreating into procedure.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of the chapter9-10 command-refusal squeeze on both Silvermoon and Kirin Tor sides.
beatAudit.transition: Override-signal coherently hands the chapter from political collapse into the Jaina emergency of beat 11.

beatAudit.pacing: High closing hook; the beat ends on a decisive redirect rather than on explanation.
beatAudit.character: Sylvanas reprioritizes instantly, proving the missing partner thread now outranks the prince thread in field urgency.
beatAudit.continuity: Evolution of chapter10's Caer Darrow descent and the damaged partnership state, kept strictly to external observation.
beatAudit.transition: Parallel-handoff is coherent: chapter12 remains inside Jaina's room while chapter13 inherits Sylvanas's northbound arrival pressure.

pacing.notes: The chapter climbs from altered formation to bad mobilization to political squeeze to redirect without losing forward pull. Sequel beats 1, 3, 8, and 10 provide enough breathing room to keep the camp and aftermath runs legible. Beat 10 was checked as the vulnerable spot and now lands as its own consequence before beat 11 takes the hook.

structure.notes: Scene-sequel balance sits at 7 scene beats to 4 sequels, appropriate for an action-heavy bridge chapter. The midchapter turn lands at beats 6-8 when the correct briefing becomes the wrong mobilization. Arc promises are earned: the opening shows Sylvanas still half betting on the right human lever, the chapter test happens through Arthas and Uther, and the close kills the border fiction in practice by keeping her inside Lordaeron's collapse.

character.notes: POV remains fully inside Sylvanas's perception. She makes the operative choices at beats 1, 3, 6, 8, 10, and 11, so the chapter never turns passive. Supporting cast stay distinct: Cyndia as damaged flank continuity, Falric as protocol friction, Arthas as dangerous instrument, Uther as truth-teller, and Lor'themar as collapsing political shield.

transitions.notes: All 11 transitionIntent entries were verified for coverage and coherence. No beat was missing a transition intent. Weakest carries rechecked: beat 4 to 5 (camp read to gate friction), beat 9 to 10 (mobilization aftermath to political squeeze), and beat 10 to 11 (institutional collapse to emergency override). One intentional break remains at beat 11 for the chapter11/chapter12 parallel cut.

continuity.notes: 2 beats are new and 9 are evolutions; callback density stays at 0%, which fits a forward-driving parallel chapter. No contradictions were found on geography, spellings, or timeline state. Two anti-reintroduction guards were added: Arthas is treated as an evolution of the chapter6 refusal rather than a fresh prince-scene, and Jaina's Caer Darrow state is kept strictly as an external continuation of chapter10 rather than a new interior reveal.

modifications.description: Aligned beat 2 to yes-but so its result shape matches the actual goal/outcome logic: the riders keep moving, but the land proves already lost.
Strengthened beat 10 so recall pressure and erased evidence land as their own consequence, ensuring the chapter earns Sylvanas's end-state shift before the redirect north.
Locked beat 11 and the chapter bridge to external observation only so the parallel cut from chapter10 does not imply Sylvanas knows anything about Jaina's interior scene below Caer Darrow.
